name: Skyros
intro: Skyros is the largest and most isolated of the Sporades, geographically closer to Euboea but culturally a world of its own. Its two halves are dramatically different: a barren, rocky south where wild Skyrian horses roam (a distinct breed, smaller than ponies), and a lush, wooded north with Skyros Town, the prettiest village in the Sporades. In antiquity, this is where Achilles was hidden before Troy, and in 1915 where the English poet Rupert Brooke was buried.
subtitle: Wild horses, poet's graves, and a unique cultural isolation

itinerary.title: 3-day Itinerary for Skyros
itinerary.subtitle: Wild horses, poet's graves, and a unique cultural isolation

stops.name: Linaria (port)
stops.desc: Tiny pretty port on the west coast. Arrival tradition: a ship's horn salutes every ferry. One hour from the main town.

stops.name: Skyros Town (Chora)
stops.desc: Cycladic-style whitewashed village on a cliff, unique to the Sporades. The main marble-paved pedestrian street climbs to the castle.

stops.name: Byzantine Castle of Skyros
stops.desc: Ancient acropolis turned Venetian/Byzantine fort. 360° views over the town and the Aegean.

stops.name: Lunch — Anemos
stops.desc: Small taverna with panoramic terrace. Excellent lobster pasta (Skyros is famous for lobster).

stops.name: Faltaits Folklore Museum
stops.desc: In a 19th-century mansion. Skyrian embroidery, carved wooden furniture, Byzantine icons. Best folk museum in Greece.

stops.name: Magazia Beach (sunset)
stops.desc: Long sandy beach right below Chora. Walk down for a swim before dinner.

stops.name: Molos Beach
stops.desc: Continuation of Magazia beach. Organized with umbrellas, tavernas; becomes wilder further north.

stops.name: Palamari Archaeological Site
stops.desc: Early Bronze Age settlement (2500 BC). Small but important site with defensive walls still visible.

stops.name: Atsitsa Beach
stops.desc: Pine-shaded pebble beach on the west coast. Hippy vibe, yoga retreat nearby. Lunch at the beach taverna.

stops.name: Pefkos Beach
stops.desc: Shallow sandy bay backed by a pine forest. Calm, family-friendly.

stops.name: Kalamitsa Beach
stops.desc: Beautiful sandy bay 10 min from Linaria. Shallow water, good for a morning swim.

stops.name: Rupert Brooke's Grave
stops.desc: Isolated olive grove at Tris Boukes bay where the English WWI poet was buried in 1915. Quiet, moving. Rough dirt track for the last 2 km.

stops.name: Skyrian Horse reserve
stops.desc: See the island's unique native pony breed — among the smallest horses in the world, nearly extinct. Volunteers run a small sanctuary open to visitors.

stops.name: Back to Linaria
stops.desc: Return to port. Coffee at Almyra before your ferry.

beaches.name: Magazia & Molos
beaches.desc: Skyros' main beach strip below Chora. Organized section in the middle, quieter at both ends.

beaches.name: Atsitsa
beaches.desc: Pine forest runs down to the sea. Clean water, unusual setting for the Aegean.

beaches.name: Kalamitsa
beaches.desc: Calm sandy beach 10 min from the port. Mix of organized and wild, family-friendly.
